name: Sentry REST API
what_it_does: Error tracking and performance monitoring REST API for querying issues, events, releases, and projects — enabling automated triage, alerting, and integration of Sentry error data into agent workflows.
use_cases: Automated issue triage — agents querying Sentry for new unresolved errors and prioritizing by frequency
Release health monitoring — checking error rates after deployments via API
Creating and updating issues programmatically (e.g., marking issues resolved after a code fix is deployed)
Integrating Sentry error data into incident response workflows
Building custom dashboards over Sentry issues and performance data
not_for: Teams not using Sentry for error monitoring
Real-time streaming of errors (Sentry webhooks provide near-real-time; true streaming requires separate approach)
Infrastructure metrics monitoring (use Datadog or Prometheus for that)
Application performance profiling at code-path level (Sentry does this but APM tools do it better)
best_when: Your team uses Sentry and you want agents to query, triage, or act on error data without using the MCP server — particularly for server-side automation rather than IDE-embedded workflows.
avoid_when: You want an IDE-embedded agent experience — use the sentry-mcp for that. Or if your team uses a different error tracking platform.

auth.notes: User Auth Tokens with specific scopes (org:read, project:read, event:write, etc.). Internal integrations use auth tokens; public integrations use OAuth with install flow. Scopes are additive — request only what you need.

pricing.paid_tiers: Team from $26/month for 50K errors; Business from $80/month; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Free tier is usable for small projects. API access on all plans. Paid plans significantly increase event quotas and add features like advanced search and retention.

performance.rate_limits: 100 requests/second per token on paid plans; lower on free

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Well-structured REST API with consistent JSON error format. Permission errors clearly indicate missing scope. Rate limit responses include retry-after header.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Issue status updates (resolve, ignore) are idempotent. Event ingestion via SDK is not idempotent. API reads are fully idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Token scopes must be explicitly requested — missing scopes return 403 with clear message but require token regeneration
Organization slug and project slug required for most endpoints — agents must resolve names to slugs
Issue queries use Sentry's proprietary search syntax (is:unresolved, times_seen:>10) — not standard SQL
Rate limits are per-token, not per-IP — shared tokens across agents can hit limits
Event data retention varies by plan — historical events may not be available
DSN (client-side) and API tokens (server-side) are completely different auth mechanisms — don't confuse them
